content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "field-operations",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 32,
    "domain_depth": 25,
    "data_richness": 18,
    "interaction_quality": 22,
    "ai_integration": 10,
    "mobile_first": 15,
    "performance": 35,
    "typescript_quality": 40,
    "error_handling": 18,
    "realtime": 20,
    "accessibility": 15,
    "security": 25,
    "differentiation": 12,
    "enterprise_readiness": 15
  },
  "overall_score": 22,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C4-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Daily Log page missing weather auto-population, crew quick-fill, and legally defensible structure",
      "file": "src/pages/DailyLog.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 7 requires weather auto-population from API, 'Same as yesterday' crew clone, manpower table by trade/company/headcount/hours, voice-to-text, calendar view showing log gaps, and draft/submitted/approved status workflow. Current page lacks all of these — it shows a basic form without weather integration, no crew pre-fill, no status workflow, and no calendar view. A superintendent cannot submit a complete log in under 90 seconds.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, completely rebuild the DailyLog component to implement the P0-4 Daily Log Speed Optimization from PRODUCTION_ROADMAP.md. BEFORE: The page renders a basic form with manual text inputs, no weather auto-population, no crew quick-fill, no status workflow (draft/submitted/approved), no calendar view, and no 'Same as yesterday' button. AFTER: The page should have: (1) A calendar sidebar showing which days have logs (green=approved, yellow=submitted, orange=draft, red=missing) using the existing `daily_logs` table with `log_date` and `status` columns. (2) Weather auto-populated on load by reading from the `weather_cache` table for the current project (show temperature high/low, conditions, wind with an 'Auto' badge — allow manual override). (3) A 'Same as yesterday' button that clones crew entries, equipment, and visitors from the previous day's log. (4) Manpower section as a table: trade, company, headcount, hours worked, with a running total row at bottom. (5) Toggle defaults: 'No incidents today' ON by default (section hidden unless toggled off), 'No visitors today' ON by default. (6) A sticky 'Submit Log' button at the bottom. (7) Status workflow: logs start as 'draft', superintendent clicks 'Submit for Approval' to move to 'submitted' (locks editing), PM approves to move to 'approved' (permanently locked). (8) Use metric cards at top: Total Logs This Month, Days Without Incident, Average Crew Size, Missing Log Days. (9) Use the theme tokens from src/styles/theme.ts for all styling (inline styles, no CSS modules). (10) Query data from Supabase using the existing supabase client from src/lib/supabase.ts. Use useEffect to fetch daily_logs for the current project, and show skeleton loaders while loading. (11) Empty state: 'No daily logs yet. The daily log is your project official record.' with CTA 'Start Today Log'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C4-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Safety page missing TRIR calculation, incident severity workflow, and OSHA compliance",
      "file": "src/pages/Safety.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 11 requires: Days Since Last Incident counter (resets on medical_treatment+ severity), TRIR calculation from incident/manpower data, corrective action tracking with overdue notifications, toolbox talk attendance with digital sign-in, certification tracking with 90/60/30 day expiry alerts, and inspection templates. Current Safety page is either missing or shows placeholder content without any of these construction-specific safety workflows.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, rebuild the Safety page component to match PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 11. BEFORE: The page shows basic placeholder content without TRIR calculation, incident severity color coding, corrective actions, toolbox talks, certification tracking, or inspection templates. AFTER: The page should have: (1) Dashboard metric cards at top: 'Days Since Last Incident' (prominent, large number — resets to 0 on severity >= medical_treatment; first_aid does not reset), TRIR calculated as (Total Recordable Incidents x 200000) / Total Hours Worked, Open Corrective Actions count, Inspections This Week, Certifications Expiring Soon. (2) Tab navigation: Incidents | Inspections | Toolbox Talks | Certifications. (3) Incidents tab: table with Date, Type, Severity (first_aid=yellow, medical_treatment=orange, lost_time=red, fatality=black — these colors are immutable per OSHA), Location, Involved Parties, Status (open/investigating/closed). Each incident links to corrective actions. (4) Inspections tab: list of upcoming and past inspections with pass/fail counts. (5) Toolbox Talks tab: list showing topic, date, presenter, attendee count. (6) Certifications tab: personnel certifications with expiry dates — red 'EXPIRED' badge if past, amber warning if within 30 days. (7) Query from Supabase tables: incidents, safety_inspections, toolbox_talks, safety_certifications, corrective_actions. Show skeleton loaders while fetching. (8) Empty state per tab: e.g., 'No incidents recorded. Safety tracking not yet configured.' with setup CTA. (9) All styling via inline styles using theme tokens from src/styles/theme.ts. No hyphens in UI text. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C4-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "Field Capture page missing camera-first workflow, GPS tagging, and AI photo analysis",
      "file": "src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 14 requires: camera-first UX (Capture button opens camera immediately), auto-metadata (GPS, timestamp, weather), post-capture form with optional title/notes/tags/linking, drawing pin placement, AI photo analysis for safety violations, offline capture queue, and time-lapse view. Current page is missing or has minimal implementation without any of these field-first workflows.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx, rebuild the FieldCapture page to match PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 14 and PRODUCTION_ROADMAP field-first requirements. BEFORE: The page shows minimal content without camera integration, GPS tagging, photo feed, map view, or AI analysis indicators. AFTER: The page should have: (1) Metric cards at top: Total Captures, Captures Today, Pending Upload (offline queue count), AI Flags. (2) A prominent 'Capture' button (large, orange, centered or fixed at bottom on mobile) that triggers a file input with accept='image/*' and capture='environment' attributes for mobile camera access. (3) Photo feed: chronological grid of captured photos showing thumbnail, timestamp, GPS badge, and linked item indicator. Use CSS grid with 3 columns on desktop, 2 on tablet, 1 on mobile. (4) Post-capture overlay form: after selecting/capturing a photo, show a form with optional Title, Notes (textarea), Tags (multi-select from predefined construction tags like 'progress', 'safety', 'defect', 'delivery'), and 'Link to' searchable dropdown (drawing/task/punch/RFI/daily log). All fields optional — user can save with just the photo. (5) Map view toggle: button to switch between grid and map view. Map view plots photos by GPS coordinates using a simple placeholder map (show lat/lng text if Mapbox not configured). (6) Each photo card shows: thumbnail, formatted timestamp, location text, tag pills, linked item badge, and an AI sparkle icon if AI has flagged something. (7) Query from Supabase: select from a 'field_captures' or 'files' table filtered by project_id and category='photo'. Show skeleton loaders while loading. (8) Empty state: 'No field captures yet. Start documenting site conditions with photos.' with camera icon illustration and 'Open Camera' CTA. (9) Offline indicator banner: 'You are offline. Photos will sync when you reconnect.' shown when navigator.onLine is false. (10) All styling inline using theme tokens. Touch targets minimum 44x44px. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C4-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "All field operations pages use mock data instead of Supabase queries",
      "file": "src/hooks/useDailyLogs.ts",
      "description": "Founder priority P0 states 'Replace ALL mock data imports in pages with real useSupabase hooks. Every list page should query Supabase.' The daily log, safety, and field capture pages either use hardcoded mock arrays or have no data hooks at all. Need a proper useDailyLogs hook following the codebase pattern from CODEBASE_PATTERNS.md that queries the daily_logs table with real-time subscription.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/hooks/useDailyLogs.ts, create a new React hook called useDailyLogs that follows the exact hook pattern from CODEBASE_PATTERNS.md. BEFORE: This file either does not exist or returns hardcoded mock data. AFTER: The hook should: (1) Import supabase from '../lib/supabase'. (2) Accept a projectId string parameter. (3) Maintain state: data (DailyLog array), isLoading (boolean), error (Error or null). (4) On mount and when projectId changes, query supabase.from('daily_logs').select('*').eq('project_id', projectId).order('log_date', { ascending: false }). (5) Set up a real-time subscription on the daily_logs table filtered by project_id using supabase.channel('daily_logs_changes').on('postgres_changes', ...).subscribe(). On any change event, refetch the data. (6) Clean up the channel subscription on unmount. (7) Export a create function that inserts into daily_logs with project_id, log_date, status='draft', weather fields, and created_by from the current auth user. (8) Export an update function for changing status (draft->submitted->approved). (9) Type the DailyLog interface with fields matching the daily_logs table schema: id, project_id, log_date, weather_description, temperature_high, temperature_low, weather_notes, site_visit_notes, safety_incidents, safety_notes, visitor_count, status (draft/submitted/approved), created_by, created_at, updated_at. (10) When the database is empty, isLoading should be false and data should be an empty array (the page component handles empty states). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C4-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "ai_integration",
      "title": "No AI daily summary generation or voice-to-text in field operations",
      "file": "src/components/dailylog/AIDailySummary.tsx",
      "description": "PRODUCTION_ROADMAP P0-4 requires voice-to-text for work description fields and the MODULE_SPECIFICATIONS require 'AI summary: generates a one-paragraph summary of the day for the PM.' Neither feature exists. The ai-daily-summary edge function is specified in BACKEND_ARCHITECTURE but no frontend component invokes it. This is a key differentiator vs Procore.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/dailylog/AIDailySummary.tsx, create a new React component called AIDailySummary that provides AI-powered daily log summarization. BEFORE: This file does not exist. There is no AI summary generation in the daily log workflow. AFTER: The component should: (1) Accept props: projectId (string), logDate (string), workSummary (string), crewCount (number), weatherDescription (string), safetyIncidents (number), onSummaryGenerated (callback with summary string). (2) Render a card with a sparkle icon and 'Generate AI Summary' button styled with theme.colors.primaryOrange background, white text, 8px border radius. (3) When clicked, set a loading state (show spinner replacing button text, same button width). (4) Call supabase.functions.invoke('ai-daily-summary', { body: { projectId, logDate, field_notes: workSummary, crew_count: crewCount, temperature_high: null, temperature_low: null } }). (5) On success, display the generated summary in a styled text block with a light orange background (rgba of primaryOrange at 0.05), 12px border radius, 16px padding. Show a 'Copy' button and an 'Insert into Log' button that calls onSummaryGenerated. (6) On error, show a toast-style error message: 'AI summary unavailable. Try again later.' with a retry button. Do not crash the page. (7) If the edge function does not exist yet, handle the 404 gracefully with the same error message. (8) Add a voice-to-text section below: a microphone icon button that, when clicked, uses the Web Speech API (window.SpeechRecognition or webkitSpeechRecognition) to transcribe speech. Show a pulsing red dot while recording. On speech end, call onSummaryGenerated with the transcribed text. If Speech API is not available, hide the microphone button entirely. (9) All styling inline using theme tokens. No hyphens in UI text. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "Crew GPS Heat Map with Geofence Auto Check-in",
      "description": "Using the existing crew_gps_locations and time_entries tables, show a real-time heat map of crew presence across the site. When a worker enters the project geofence (per project_location table), automatically log a check-in to time_entries. Superintendents see which areas have activity and which are idle. This replaces manual sign-in sheets and catches no-shows instantly.",
      "effort": "medium"
    },
    {
      "title": "Photo Progress Time-lapse with AI Delta Detection",
      "description": "Group field capture photos by GPS location across dates. Display as a time-lapse slider showing construction progress at each spot. AI analyzes consecutive photos to detect and annotate what changed (new framing visible, concrete poured, drywall installed). Generates automatic progress percentage updates linked to schedule activities.",
      "effort": "large"
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's Quick Capture is their strongest field feature — video to punch item with automatic transcription. Fieldwire has true offline-first design with Apple Pencil markup and QR code integration for physical-to-digital linking. SiteSync's field operations module currently falls far behind both: no camera-first workflow, no offline support, no voice capture, no GPS tagging. The daily log lacks the speed optimizations (same-as-yesterday, weather auto-fill, toggle defaults) that would make it competitive with even basic paper workflows. Opusense AI specifically targets voice-to-text field reports — SiteSync needs this to compete. Buildots uses helmet cameras for automatic progress tracking — the photo time-lapse feature would be a step toward this without requiring specialized hardware.",
  "summary": "Field Operations is the weakest module at 22/100 and represents the biggest risk to product adoption. Construction superintendents live in the field — if daily logging takes more than 90 seconds, if safety tracking lacks OSHA compliance, and if photo capture requires more than one tap, they will not use SiteSync. The five issues target the three field operations pages (Daily Log, Safety, Field Capture) with complete rebuilds following PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA exactly, plus the critical data layer (useDailyLogs hook wired to Supabase instead of mock data) and AI integration (daily summary generation and voice-to-text). The Daily Log rebuild is the single highest-impact change in the entire application — it is the page a superintendent uses every single day, and the PRODUCTION_ROADMAP marks it as P0-4 with a specific 90-second completion benchmark. Without these changes, no superintendent will adopt SiteSync over their current paper or Procore workflow."
}
```
